item.title: Meta Launches Muse, an Image Model Built Around Your Own Photos
item.description: Photo restoration, Renaissance-portrait style transfer, claymation, room restyling, product shots — Muse turns your personal photo library into the raw material, putting Meta AI squarely against Adobe Firefly and Google Imagen.

item.title: The Sora Countdown: App Already Gone, API Sunsets September 24
item.description: OpenAI closed the Sora app on April 26 and the API dies September 24. If Sora clips are anywhere in your pipeline, this is your 11-week migration window — reviewers have already moved it to the 'legacy' column.

item.title: Midjourney V8.1 Becomes the Default: Native 2K, 4–5x Faster, Draft Mode
item.description: The new default model ships native 2K HD output, a big speed jump, better prompt adherence, and a Draft Mode built for rapid ideation before you commit render credits to a final.

item.title: July Video Rankings: Runway Gen-4.5 Owns Control, Veo 3.1 Owns Realism
item.description: The month's big comparison crowns Runway Gen-4.5 the pro-workflow pick — camera direction, structured prompting, music videos and previs — while Veo 3.1 and Seedance 2.5 lead on raw realism. Kling 3.0 Turbo and Pika stay the daily drivers.

item.title: Gemini Omni Flash Prices Fast Video Iteration at $0.10 per Second
item.description: Google's conversational video model is built for quick back-and-forth edits rather than one-shot renders — a different bet on how creators actually work, and cheap enough to iterate freely.

item.title: Beat-Sync Goes Mainstream: Full AI Music Videos From a Finished Track
item.description: Tools like Freebeat and Neural Frames now analyze BPM and transients to cut visuals to beat drops, hold character consistency, and lip-sync avatars across 6-minute videos — indie artists are shipping label-grade visuals on no budget.

item.title: Kling O3 Brings 15-Second Multi-Shot Scenes With Voice-Persistent Characters
item.description: Multi-shot generation, character voices that persist across cuts, and native audio push Kling toward short-film territory rather than clip stitching.

item.title: Copyright Consensus Forming: AI-Assisted Work Is Protectable — With Receipts
item.description: Many jurisdictions now extend protection to AI-assisted works when there's demonstrable human-in-the-loop direction: custom prompting, manual editing, creative decisions. Practical takeaway — keep your prompt and edit history.

item.title: Fable 5 Goes Pay-Per-Use Today — Writers, Check Your Stack
item.description: Claude Fable 5 now costs $10/M input and $50/M output tokens on every subscriber tier, while Sonnet 5 ($2/$10 intro) and Opus 4.8 stay bundled. For long-form drafting, the bundled models are the value play.

item.title: GPT Image 2: Natural-Language Edits, 2K Output, Multilingual Text
item.description: OpenAI's image model has become the instruction-follower of the field — revise existing visuals and apply targeted edits by just describing them, with text rendering that finally survives non-English scripts.

item.title: CapCut's One-Workspace Play: Seedance 2.0 Video, GPT Image 2, and Seedmusic
item.description: The everything-suite pitch is landing with small content teams: video generation, image editing, and AI music in one workflow, ending the tool-switching tax between five subscriptions.

item.title: Cloudflare's September 15 Deadline Splits Search Crawlers From AI Crawlers
item.description: After the cutoff, mixed-use bots get blocked by default from ad-carrying pages. If you publish content, this is the biggest default-setting change yet in who gets to train on your work.

item.title: AI Actor Tilly Norwood Lands Her First Feature Film
item.description: 'Misaligned,' a coming-of-age story about a girl AI bot set in the Tillyverse, makes the synthetic performer's leap to features official — and reopens every argument about human performers at once.

item.title: Wan 2.7 Is the Open Option for Local and Custom Video Pipelines
item.description: If local control or custom pipelines matter more than leaderboard wins, Wan remains the meaningful open-weight choice — structure-preserving transforms and no per-second meter running.

item.title: The Model Buffet: Platforms Now Bundle 14+ Image Models Under One License
item.description: Artlist's toolkit stacks Nano Banana, GPT Image 2, Seedream 5.0, Wan 2.7 and more behind one subscription with commercial licensing sorted — a hedge against betting your whole aesthetic on one model.

item.title: Grok Imagine Video 1.5 Adds Native Synced Audio and Faster Image-to-Video
item.description: A real contender for social-first output if you already live inside X — quick-turn clips with sound baked in, tuned for the feed rather than the timeline.

item.title: 'Acting Prompts' Arrive: Performance Capture Synthesis Maps Vocals to Avatars
item.description: The new wave of lip-sync tools reads vocal nuance and maps it onto digital performers — direct emotional transitions by timestamp ('melancholy to euphoria at 02:15') like a VFX house, from your phone.

item.title: One Week In: Claude Sonnet 5 Is the New Default for Long-Form Drafting
item.description: Launched July 1 at $2/$10 introductory pricing and bundled into subscriptions, Sonnet 5 has quickly become the workhorse recommendation for novelists, scriptwriters and newsletter writers.

item.title: Anthropic Overtakes OpenAI on Revenue for the First Time
item.description: A $47B annualized run rate versus OpenAI's $25–33B. The broader signal for creators: the money is consolidating around a handful of labs whose pricing decisions now ripple straight into your tool subscriptions.
